title: Pea & Ham Soup

overview.description: A traditional British soup combining sweet peas with smoky ham in a smooth, comforting bowl that's perfect with a cheese sandwich.

method: Melt the butter in a large saucepan over a medium heat.
Add the onion and cook gently for about 8 minutes until softened without colouring.
Add the garlic and cook for 1 minute.
Pour in the stock and bring to a gentle simmer.
Add the frozen peas and cook for 5 minutes until just tender.
Stir in three-quarters of the ham.
Return the soup to the pan, stir in the double cream if using and season to taste.
Serve topped with the remaining diced ham and a little chopped mint if desired.

learning.did-you-know: Pea and ham soup has been a staple of British kitchens for generations, originally made to use up leftover ham from a Sunday roast or boiled gammon.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How frozen peas preserve their sweetness, why soups made with peas only need a short cooking time, and how leftovers from other meals can become a completely new dish.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: A fantastic economical soup that makes excellent use of leftover ham while relying on freezer staples. Quick enough for a Thursday evening and ideal for batch cooking.
chapman-tips: Don't overcook the peas or they'll lose their bright colour and fresh flavour.
Ham stock gives the richest flavour, but homemade chicken stock works beautifully too.
Keep some diced ham back for serving to add texture.
notes.batch-cooking: Freeze in family-sized portions for up to three months.
notes.leftovers: Reheat gently, adding a splash of stock if the soup thickens after chilling.
